TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Tsou, story: TsouConv-typhoon

S.FORM: 'o vcongʉsi to ha hanako ci ihe ne spukunu ne noana'o au.
S.TRANSL: 以前hanako的先生在Spukunu那裡。
In the past, Hanako's husband was in Spukunu.

S.FORM: 'a 'a moso la no maezo fiho uh ne'e moso la ehtothomʉ.
S.TRANSL: 以前戰爭的時候，那邊的人也有跟著去(打仗)
In the war, people there also followed (to fight).

S.FORM: eh uh ho la uh ne'e lasi acnia engha X.
S.TRANSL: 我每次去那邊他每次必定都講(這件事)。
Every time I went there, he definitely told (such a story).

S.FORM: zou mai ho la 'a 'so no eainca ntoho la mici m'ene ne maaya no na ino peisu.
S.TRANSL: 比方說， 他們曾經曾經想要向日本要錢。
For example, they had been considering to ask for money from Japan.

S.FORM: ia zou honci eainca 'a la e eh si av'a no.
S.TRANSL: 對. 如果說... 該怎麼說....?
Yes. If.... How should we say...?

S.FORM: o'a ihe no fii.
S.TRANSL: 他們沒有給(錢)嗎？
They didn't give (them money)?

S.FORM: eh bochio ihe nana fii ho.
S.TRANSL: 據說他們給了。
It is heard that they gave some.

S.FORM: 'a ohe acʉha 'a ohe acʉha nenusi no mo yu yungefi ho oh ia mo n'a ako la unto.
S.TRANSL: 不曉得他們到底是不是嫌不夠，他們還在不斷的運動當中。
Perhaps they complained the money insufficient, so they still keep on exercising.

S.FORM: ahoi ne auyusi ne an ancu ho la moso la ahoi to moso eh maezo yuyafo ho unto no enca te m'eovei.
S.TRANSL: 最初, 還沒有說XXX,...曾經 剛開始的時候, （那邊的人）也出來參加討錢的活動。
In the beginning, when XXX had not been proposed, (people there) also stood out for the activity of asking for (the compensation money).

S.FORM: oh lasi acnia engha enivo.
S.TRANSL: 他每次都講這個。
He always talks about this.

S.FORM: oh ma mo asonʉ yungefi.
S.TRANSL: 可能是嫌少。
(They) may possibly consider it is not enough.

S.FORM: ake'a n'a fii.
S.TRANSL: 稍微再給一點。
(Just) give slightly more money...

S.FORM: Yingkaidoyola.
S.TRANSL: 應該都有(拿到錢)。
(They) should have got (such money).

S.FORM: inia no 'o nia no.
S.TRANSL: 以前的那個....
In the past, the....

S.FORM: i'o i'o m i'o moso mcoi ua ohke cu fii.
S.TRANSL: 那時候死的是有給啦。
For those dead, they gave (the money) indeed.

S.FORM: mo c'o 'so at'ingi kakutia.
S.TRANSL: 但是只有很少。
But (the money was) little.

S.FORM: 'o a'ʉmtʉ 'o a'ʉmtʉ alʉ ak'i eh wodechinwaikong eh 'a ohe cu acʉha.
S.TRANSL: 我的爺爺，他們已經全部。
My real grandfathers, they are all....

S.FORM: mo moso ak'i man'i.
S.TRANSL: (錢)有沒有多一點？
Is (the money) much?

S.FORM: na a'o.
S.TRANSL: 我嗎？
Me?

S.FORM: zou.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: eh mo'u maeo to hookiyu.
S.TRANSL: 我是領薪水的。
I got salary (in the war).

S.FORM: hookiyu eh zou na nia na nia hookiyu ho moso la heitai.
S.TRANSL: 薪水，對，他是拿那時當兵的薪水。
Salary, yes, (he got) salary when he was a solider.

S.FORM: mosu s'a mosu totuiha ho mosu.
S.TRANSL: 你是...你是三年在...
For three years, you were...

S.FORM: mi'o yone'e.
S.TRANSL: 我在那裡。
I was there.

S.FORM: te n'a peela eainca ak'a ao'pova.
S.TRANSL: 還算是有稍微安撫的意思。
(That money) was somewhat able to pacify (you).

S.FORM: maka ake'a ao'po'pova.
S.TRANSL: 起碼稍微安撫一下。
(The money/The Japanese government had to somewhat pacify (you) at least.

S.FORM: ci mio no kuzo.
S.TRANSL: 這樣也沒什麼不好。
It's not so bad.

S.FORM: zou 'a mosu s'a no nia ohe niate auni no ang angumu.
S.TRANSL: 你不是差一點被洋人幹掉啊？
Weren't you almost killed by western people?

S.FORM: anna'o uk'a ci mo 'so ahtu mokalu ho mo yu'susuyumo.
S.TRANSL: 戰爭原本就沒有人真正佔到便宜。
In a war, by nature no one really takes advantage.

S.FORM: osu s'a eainca ak'i eno su nte cu c'o nte cu c'o nte cu c'o aʉmta auni no no amelika honcimu honci c'u te make.
S.TRANSL: Ak'i (外公)你不是有講過說，如果說你們失敗的話，你們幾乎要被美國人幹掉了？
Grandfather, have you ever said that, if you had really failed, you would almost have been killed by the Americans?

S.FORM: ma o'a te cu c'o ah 'ana te s'a mako yikeyuei honcimza ako ma'nao isi cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們一直逗留在那邊，不到一個月日本人已經被打敗了。
If we had kept lingering there, Japanese would have been beaten within one month.

S.FORM: n'a eansoua oh.
S.TRANSL: 我們還活著。
We are still alive.

S.FORM: ma asonʉ ancu ntoho teseupngʉ honci ntoho o'te cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.TRANSL: 如果那個時候還沒有把日本人打敗的話，戰爭還沒有結束。
If the Japanese were not defeated at that time, the war would have not yet finished.

S.FORM: zou 'a isi cu no taipuya 'o maaya micu.
S.TRANSL: 對啊，那時候已經把日本人扳倒了。
Yes, (at that time) Japanese had been knocked down.

S.FORM: pamaine'e?.
S.TRANSL: 讓你們回家嗎？（是不是就這樣子給你們回家？）
(So, they) let you go home?

S.FORM: o'tena ehtothomʉ.
S.TRANSL: 已經沒有作戰了。
There was no war.

S.FORM: zou 'a momu s'a anana'o zua make?.
S.TRANSL: 你們是不是原本就存心失敗？
Didn't you deliberately intend to lose?

S.FORM: WGS: tiate atpʉtʉ.
S.TRANSL: 差一點就打贏了。
(You) almost won.

S.FORM: WGS: h'unano biteuyunu ho ako suyumo 'o maaya.
S.TRANSL: 特別是,日本人合力一直侵略(別人)。
Especially, Japanese joined force and continuously invaded (other countries).

S.FORM: nemio zou c'o maica hia okosi.
S.TRANSL: 對啊，就這麼小的（國家）。
Yes, just a small (country).

S.FORM: suyumohe.
S.TRANSL: 反而去侵略（人家）。
To our surprise, (they) invaded (other countries)

S.FORM: une cooceng ne ap c'o maica hia okosi.
S.TRANSL: 這麼小的國家。
A country so small.

S.FORM: ne apihana ohe eae taivang su su'uni ne nanyangshan ho ucia suyumi.
S.TRANSL: (日本人) 想要侵略從朝鮮到對岸，台灣，到南洋。
(Japanese) wanted to invade (the areas) Korea, the other side of the Taiwan Strait, Taiwan and Nanyang.

S.FORM: 'so a'ʉmta tau taunanavʉ na na nia cou nemio 'a.
S.TRANSL: 很欽佩當時的鄒人。
Cou people at that time should be highly respected.

S.FORM: 'a s'a anouc'o cou'a'toana 'a s'a no counecovhi eh moso maezo botngonʉ.
S.TRANSL: 不僅只是我們鄒人，別的地方的原住民應該也有很多。
Not only we Tsou, but there were also a lot of aborignes from other places.

S.FORM: acʉcʉhʉ zou acʉcʉhʉ 'o moso afu'u cou ta taivang.
S.TRANSL: 反正就是全台灣的原住民。
At least all the aborigines in Taiwan.

S.FORM: WS: cou ta acʉhʉ iachi 'a te 'ana s'a aoko maica ci seising maitan'e s sen seisicu.
S.TRANSL: 全部自己不用在一直seising... seisicu。
All people themselves don't always 'seisicu'.

S.FORM: lahe enca te'o te te'o te'o mo'unu maita'e a'lʉ dengji no enca gansidui.
S.TRANSL: 他們說我要出去甚至登記成敢死隊。
They said, I'll come forward. They even enrolled as a member of the suicide squad.

S.FORM: ia ia iachi sigan.
S.TRANSL: 自己讀士官學校。
(they) themselves (decided to go to the school of) sergeant.

S.FORM: eh te a'ʉmta eainca mo nongonongo 'o mo maica.
S.TRANSL: 像那一種的真的可以說是很笨。
(people) like this can be said really stupid.

S.FORM: ci mio 'so X.
S.TRANSL: 真的是如此。
It is indeed.

S.FORM: na'no mici na'no mici heetai.
S.TRANSL: 真的很想從軍。
(They) wanted to become a solider very much.

S.FORM: aceava eainca mo nongonongo 'o moso la eainca mo a'ʉmtʉ ia.
S.TRANSL: 真的可以全部這樣說很笨。
All these could be said stupid.

S.FORM: 'a mo a'ʉmtʉ eainca oh ho nemio te n te s'a no peela eainca mo ʉmnʉ ci ongko.
S.TRANSL: 可能不能講說那是好的名聲。
At that time, it could not be said as a good name.

S.FORM: Shia Shiaozhong ribentianhuang ia che taiyuanle.
S.TRANSL: (他們說,我要)效忠日本天皇。我離題太遠了。
(They said, I want to) pledgy loyalty to Mikado....I seriously stray from the main subject.

S.FORM: ia ia ia mako ako nomso no te ako p payoneni ta mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 給老人家住的地方不曉得是不是住得下。
(We don't know) whether (you) have enough space for the elder to stay.

S.FORM: teko nomso ak'i.
S.TRANSL: 外公你還住得下嗎？
Grandfather, can you fit-in the space?

S.FORM: 'a teno payoneni 'o omza la leoni sica.
S.TRANSL: 就給他住那邊以前我們住的地方。
Just makes him stay where we used to live.

S.FORM: ia lamu cu eon tan'e?.
S.TRANSL: 你們現在住這邊嗎？
Are you living here now?

S.FORM: 'a lamza eh s'o eainca teta nana nomso?.
S.TRANSL: 我們住這邊, (我)心想說他不曉得是不是住得下（以前的地方）
We live here....I am thinking whether he can get fit in ( that place.)

S.FORM: nomso alʉ es alʉ toe ia eainca ta'una(na) mita c'o noachi iama mita alʉ.
S.TRANSL: 以為他只是說一個人，原來他竟然是...
(I) thought he was alone. Unexpectedly, he was....

S.FORM: iama mihin'i alʉ.
S.TRANSL: 原來他們竟然是...（有好幾個人）。
Unexpectedly, they were (many people).

S.FORM: mako ma'himamso to.
S.TRANSL: 起碼（地方）要睡得下。
At least, (there should be a place) with enough space for sleeping.

S.FORM: si at'ing'a tonzovi 'o nazua 'ancu isi teapha to.
S.TRANSL: 她只是洗被裡的棉絮，還沒有裝到..
She just washed the cotton in the bedquilt, and not yet filled it into...

S.FORM: pa patmeaphi no LMJnazua 'e 'e ak'i.
S.TRANSL: 叫外公（自己）裝棉絮
Make Grandfather fill the cotton (into the bedquilt).

S.FORM: tahe la tena la zou mici eemo ne eam eaveiyo yoyohu.
S.TRANSL: 他們會..他們想要在veiyo 旁邊，在友友湖那邊。
They want to build a house around Veiyo in Yoyohu.

S.FORM: i'o avai.
S.TRANSL: 是avai嗎?
(You mean) Avai?

S.FORM: zou te s'a no ahta pa'ea honci o'a tehin'i s'a.
S.TRANSL: 如果他們不要...(蓋房子)，可能不會挖吧。
If they don't want (to build the house), they probably will not dig (it).

S.FORM: oh 'so anga 'a 'ua isi eainca te X aeo ayocʉ na yupasʉ.
S.TRANSL: 錢先預先準備好。
Money is prepared.

S.FORM: WG: ake'i X 'ancu la mai to la o'a ihin'i 'so ahta eʉsvʉta la'u no o'te meelʉ iachi mooeai to peisu.
S.TRANSL: 還沒像說，他們都沒有告訴你們說我也會製造錢。
Not like... They have never told you that I can make money?

S.FORM: to'toefʉngʉ ho mooeai peisu.
S.TRANSL: 躲起來印鈔票。
(You) hide and make money.

S.FORM: cu ahtʉ oemi'mi.
S.TRANSL: 鈔票還沒乾。
(The money bill) is not dry.

S.FORM: o'a mo oemi'mi 'o peisu.
S.TRANSL: 錢還沒有乾。
The money is not dry yet.

S.FORM: oho la 'so eainca moso la ne moso la ne moso la cmoehu ne moso la hepotaifong 'a osi la no 'so nia'te toemiza to eʉskʉ 'o ne i'o ne sngʉsngʉyo 'a isi c'o a'ʉmta nia'te.
S.TRANSL: 但是那個地方曾經差一點被賀伯颱風給掩埋。
But that place was almost covered by the landslide in He-po Typhoon. It's almost..

S.FORM: oh anova nia'te cu a'la.
S.TRANSL: 不只是差一點，甚至已經...
It's not just 'almost'; it had been...

S.FORM: ake'a n'a.
S.TRANSL: 稍微有一點...
It was somewhat...

S.FORM: pahpahsaha to lingki.
S.TRANSL: 已經被土石流，泥巴抱起來了。
(The place) has been enfolded by mud.

S.FORM: pocenga no mo XasvʉtʉX ci eatatiskova.
S.TRANSL: 還好還被.....的人眷顧著。
(Luckily they were still) looked after by XXX people.

S.FORM: 'a cu no a'ʉmta pocenga no kamisama ho mo maica.
S.TRANSL: 就是像這樣的被上帝所眷顧著。
Just like this to be looked after by God.

S.FORM: o'u la teai ci suiziva 'a o'u la no teai 'o mo ne'e os'o cu no.
S.TRANSL: 那個時候我做的廚房; 那個房子以前是我做的。我已經....
That kitchen is what I built. The house is what I built. I've...

S.FORM: to hia pepe to mato mo asonʉ.
S.TRANSL: 可能窗戶的高度...
It is possible that the height of the window...

S.FORM: te cu ak'a nia'te.
S.TRANSL: (那個水)已經差一點就要...
(The water) would almost...

S.FORM: si a'bohta iachia sngʉsnga to maukukuzo.
S.TRANSL: 好在一些草自己把水堵住。
It's luckly the water flow was clogged with some straw.

S.FORM: 'ana mo s'a man'i 'o moso toemi to cingona no hifi.
S.TRANSL: 從牆角流過去的水已經沒有很多了。
There was not much water passing by the back of the house corner.

S.FORM: oh mohcu smounuhe to maicasi.
S.TRANSL: 然後水就往另一邊流。
(And then,) the water ran to another side with landslide.

S.FORM: iachia yʉntʉhi to chumu 'o ceo'u.
S.TRANSL: 水自己把石牆沖斷。
It is water itself to rush over the stone hedge and destroy it.

S.FORM: 'so a'ʉmtʉ smomacongo.
S.TRANSL: 水真的沖得很快。
(The water) really rushed so fast.

S.FORM: 'a ntoho acʉhʉ smounu to coca na na chumu honci o'te iachia yʉntʉhi.
S.TRANSL: 水原本會全部往庭園沖，如果水沒有把石牆沖斷的話。
The water should have all rushed toward the yard, if the water didn't destroy (the stone hedge).

S.FORM: oh mais'a isi teoteaineni no la aʉt'ʉcʉ ta eatatiskova.
S.TRANSL: 好像是被專門在照顧人類的替我們弄成這樣（安排的）。
It seems to be arranged by one who especially looked after (us/people).

S.FORM: zou zou ma mo s'o cu a'ʉmntʉ nia'te eainca moso nia'te ngohcʉ 'o 'o 'o pasuya.
S.TRANSL: 真的就差那麼一點, Pasuya差一點被流走。
It's really very close... Pasuya almost drifted away (by the hard water).

S.FORM: i'o la eainca tu cumas'a.
S.TRANSL: 大家叫杜什麼？
What is Du usually called?

S.FORM: 'o mo atva'esi oko.
S.TRANSL: 是最小的（弟弟）。
(His brother who is the) youngest.

S.FORM: cumas'a 'o ongkonosi cou.
S.TRANSL: 他的鄒語名字是什麼？
What is his name in Tsou?

S.FORM: avai avai.
S.TRANSL: Avai.
Avai.

S.FORM: 'a osi 'a moso mon'i bochio ma isi cu poe'oha no no ceoa 'o phingi to sia X'a iheX n'a mon'ia peela paavi.
S.TRANSL: (Halue)沒有馬上就知道 原來土已經推擠誰的門（車門）。他們（阿海，halue, 他弟弟）沒能馬上打開（車門）。
(Halue) didn't know immediately that the door had been blocked by the earth. They (Ahay, Halue and his brother) couldn't open the door in time.

S.FORM: zou na touni maavo to to ahae.
S.TRANSL: 聽說阿海幫他(halue)開(車門)。
(Ahae) seemed to help him to open (the car door).

S.FORM: zou cu cu yuyafo.
S.TRANSL: 就出來了。
(They just) went out (of the car).

S.FORM: cu c'o no mi to skoskopna to hnoumza mi to tangku to skoskopna to hnoumza ho.
S.TRANSL: 就只有走我們家的上面，(走在)我們家上面的蓄水桶。
(They) just could walk on the above of our house, walking on the water butt, the above of the water butt of our family..

S.FORM: 'a mimza cu no nʉthʉ micu liangdian.
S.TRANSL: 我們就這樣一起走，那時候已經兩點了。
We just walked together. At that time, it was two o'clock.

M.TRANSL: 三個人以上一起走.主焦

S.FORM: zou 'a momza cu no nʉthʉ mi to ceonʉ ne'e ma micu acʉhʉ man'i 'o mo 'o'oko no eʉskʉ ho maita'e 'o kaapana.
S.TRANSL: 當時我們一起走在那個路的時候，原來已經很多小小的崩山，而且竹子是這樣子。
When we walked together on the road, there were many landslides, and the bamboo (fell) in this way.

S.FORM: ma nte mainenu ho mo coeconʉ n'a.
S.TRANSL: 這樣子走路到底要怎麼走路啊？
What should we do if we walked in this way?

S.FORM: teoteoca 'o kaapana.
S.TRANSL: 一直砍竹子。
(we) kept cutting bamboo.

S.FORM: enca moso abohtʉ mo avokngʉ moeai ceonʉ 'o 'o 'ohaesa to pasuya.
S.TRANSL: 還好pasuya他弟弟專門在前面砍路。
It's lucky that Pasuya's younger brother focused on making road (at that time).

S.FORM: zou isi c'o suingi to tendo.
S.TRANSL: 只有手電筒照路。
Just a flash light lighted upon (his road).

S.FORM: ma man ma s'a seolʉa no ake'i.
S.TRANSL: 剛好（誰）稍微...
(Someone) happened to....

S.FORM: 'o nia ba'i?.
S.TRANSL: The grandmother who passed away?
那個過世祖母？

S.FORM: zou shengbing 'a isi c'o no sviei to avai.
S.TRANSL: 她只有被Avai背著。
Yes, Avai just carried (her) with his back.

S.FORM: zou mi to yahnou to shiaolingLM.
S.TRANSL: 走到小玲家旁邊。
(we) walked near by Shiao-Ling's home.

S.FORM: iama mo eʉskʉ 'o patinsoha to ceo'ʉsi.
S.TRANSL: 原來石垣的邊角已經垮了。
Unexpectedly, the rim of the stone hedge had collapsed.

S.FORM: ha'o mi to maita'esi isi eainca to ahai.
S.TRANSL: 就從崩掉的那一邊走過。
(we) just walked across the side with collapse.

S.FORM: mi tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 阿海說：「走這邊！」
A-hai said: Walk this way!

S.FORM: te c'o amuau bosifou to omza ta'e.
S.TRANSL: 我們就突然登上那邊的上面。
(we) just abruptly climbed up above (the stone wall.)

S.FORM: 'a i'o no omza to omza to 'o'ocea to nia 'o'ocea'u.
S.TRANSL: 就是我以前茶園的上面。
(It is) just above where my tea garden used to be.

S.FORM: zou i'o i'o mo oko no ceonʉ.
S.TRANSL: 是啦，就是那條小條的路。
Yes, (it is ) the small road.

S.FORM: ma oho n'a mihna pa'ea Xte spʉc'ʉhʉX ta kali 'o linki.
S.TRANSL: (因為這邊)剛剛挖，泥巴淹到膝蓋。
It's because (this place) was just digged. The mud went as knee's height.

S.FORM: acʉha apeoha 'o makukucu ho sulipa cu c'o hatasi.
S.TRANSL: （我們這些人）全部鞋子，拖鞋都掉了，全部都光腳。
(Of all of us,) our shoes or slippers were all gone. We were all barefoot.

S.FORM: pafiho to ha'o yusngʉsngʉcʉ to kakapana.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣子從竹林裡面抄近路。
(We) just made a short cut across bamboo forest.

S.FORM: zou bohsifou to pan to 'so ceonʉ to omzasi.
S.TRANSL: 登上-- 因為那個上面有路。
(We) ascended... because above there was a road.

S.FORM: zou 'o la'u pamo zou 'o la o'u cu ahoi bohngʉ cu yozi ne mimza cu sʉc'ʉhʉ to kesa.
S.TRANSL: 我們到kesa的時候已經四點了。
When we reached Kesa's place, (it's getting lighter and everything) started (to become) recognizable, and it's four a.m.

S.FORM: 'iama ihe sicngia 'o chumu mo uh to moohe to novuko.
S.TRANSL: 原來他們正在把水撈出, (因為)水流到novuku的房子。
Actually they were scooping up water. The water went into Novuku's house.

S.FORM: lea ʉmʉmnʉ 'o ba'i.
S.TRANSL: Ba'i(祖母)身體健康嗎？
How is Grand Mother?

S.FORM: mio.
S.TRANSL: 好。
Fine.

S.FORM: lea ʉmʉmnʉ 'o ba'i.
S.TRANSL: Ba'i(祖母)身體健康嗎？
How is Grand Mother?

S.FORM: lea no o'te na'no.
S.TRANSL: 是有啦。
Yes.

S.FORM: noana'va o'tena ahta aiti.
S.TRANSL: 很久沒有看到(她)。
(I) didn't see (her) for a long time.

S.FORM: la i ia 'o 'o ba'e.
S.TRANSL: 哪一個ba'e？
Which ba'e?

S.FORM: SGW: zou ba'e.
S.TRANSL: 對，那個老的祖母。
Yes, the old Grandmother.

S.FORM: zou 'o ba'e mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 對，那個年紀很大的ba'i。
Yes, that old Grandmother.

S.FORM: M te ho te la yon no mo botngonu ho te la.
S.TRANSL: 如果說她在人多...
When (she) stays where many people are...

S.FORM: zou la kaebʉ 'ia lahe 'so eaeza X kaeba ho la pocenga to mo botngonʉ.
S.TRANSL: 她很喜歡很多人（看）照顧著她。
She likes that many people look after her.

S.FORM: ako yon tan'e la ako mici eahioa.
S.TRANSL: （老ba'i）一直在這邊 ... 一直想要工作。
(She) is always here around...(She) always wants to work.

S.FORM: 'a e 'a eno aʉmtʉ.
S.TRANSL: 真的。
It is true.

S.FORM: maezo no sia.
S.TRANSL: 就像誰一樣。
Like somebody.

S.FORM: m o'a mici amzocni ta moo ho mo eon tan'e.
S.TRANSL: (她)在這裡的時候 (她不)想在房子裡休息。
(She doesn't) want to rest in the house when she stays here.

S.FORM: 'e mo mameoi (Exhale) mo ahmohmo hoci ako.
S.TRANSL: 老人家如果一直閒在家裡會很不習慣。
For elders, if (they) keep staying at home and doing nothing, (they will) feel unaccustomed.

S.FORM: zou la aot'ot'o'u c'o mici c'o biavovei.
S.TRANSL: （老人家）喜歡活動活動，只想到處走來走去。
(The elders love to be) active..(they only) want to...just walk around.

S.FORM: zou 'a la no maica.
S.TRANSL: 就是這樣子。
Just like this.

S.FORM: mh ma s'a mo mon'i mameoi ho mo ho mo o'te aot'ou.
S.TRANSL: 如果都沒有活動的話，很容易老化。
When (one) doesn't move, (he is) soon to get old.

S.FORM: ho mo o'te ake'i aot'ot'ou.
S.TRANSL: 如果不稍微活動活動...
When (one) doesn't move...

S.FORM: tma'congo c'u amzo mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 很快就要老去了（將近老化）。
(One) is soon to get old.

S.FORM: toehʉnga atva'si mameoi.
S.TRANSL: (他是)最老的。
(She is) the eldest.

S.FORM: atvaesi mameoi ta panno mo n'a 'so ahtu mameoihe ta 'a a zou.
S.TRANSL: 她是最老的.... 已經沒有人比他更老。
(She is) the eldest... There is no (one who) is elder than (she).

S.FORM: ta cou cou cou cou a'toana.
S.TRANSL: 我們鄒族。
of we Tsou people.

S.FORM: 'a mo atva'esi mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 最老的。
(She) is the eldest.

S.FORM: mh 'o 'o ino to nia can'e.
S.TRANSL: 就以前詹仔的媽媽。
(The one is) Can'e's mother in the past.

S.FORM: oh mo mameoihe.
S.TRANSL: 喔 原來(她)比較老。
Oh! (She) is much elder.

S.FORM: 'a mo mameoihe 'o ino to nia zaning can'e to ne ne meoina meoina.
S.TRANSL: 石壁的那個詹仔的媽媽是比較老。
The mother of Can'e in Meoina is elder (than she).

S.FORM: 'ua os'o cohivi.
S.TRANSL: 對，我知道。
Yeah, I know...

S.FORM: 'o mameoi 'a mo n'a 'a mo yoso 'o mo mameoihe oh puutu.
S.TRANSL: 還有....是有兩個比較老的... 漢人。
There are still two Han people who are much elder.

S.FORM: eh panto mo mameoi ci ea ne ootei.
S.TRANSL: 湖底那邊有一個很老的。
There is one elder person in Ootei.

S.FORM: SW: 'a mo no mo no yoso 'o mo mameoihe ci puutu.
S.TRANSL: 對，有兩個漢人很老的。
Yes, two Han people are much elder.

S.FORM: zou cu 'e ba'i at'inghi ta cou.
S.TRANSL: 然後就是ba'i了.但是在鄒族來講...
And then Ba'i. But of Tsou...

S.FORM: 'so yoayokeo 'e ba'i.
S.TRANSL: 但是Ba'i 很健朗。
But Ba'i is very healthy.

S.FORM: ita cou 'a aʉlʉ 'o ba'i 'o mo atvaesi no mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 在鄒 ba'i剛好是最老的。
Of Tsou, it is Ba'i who is the eldest.

S.FORM: koicʉ ne oho la o'te eoho'a ho patoa 'ocea.
S.TRANSL: 以前你沒有叫他來採茶他會罵人。
(In the past) she scolded (people) when (people) didn't call her and make her pick tea leave.

S.FORM: 'e 'o'oko lahin'i maka eʉsvʉta ho la toa 'ocea.
S.TRANSL: (很討厭)孩子們採茶的時候沒有跟他講。
The children didn't call her when picking tea leaves.

S.FORM: haciziusai ia humameoza.
S.TRANSL: 把她看得太老。
(people) recognize (her) being old.

S.FORM: All: humameoza.
S.TRANSL: 把(她)看得太老。
(people) recognize (her) being old.

S.FORM: na'no mici cocvo ho la la la amz 'a la 'a la no 'so eainca Xpa n'a ehoiX si mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 先讓老人家講話。
Habitually, the elders speak first.

S.FORM: 'a cua 'a mo noana'o ta'u cu teolʉi uh toa 'ocea.
S.TRANSL: 沒有多久我就會看到他去採茶。
Less than a while, I'll see (she) goes and picks tea leaves.

S.FORM: eʉsvʉta mo la i' moso la i'nʉskʉ na'no sʉ'no mza peecveni ho temza iachi toa 'ocea.
S.TRANSL: 有一次我們自己採茶，我們欺騙他，她很生氣。
One time we picked tea on our own. We cheated her. She was very angry.

S.FORM: ia moso eon to emoo mo isi ausuhza honga mo botngonʉ 'o mo biavovei.
S.TRANSL: (當時)他在家裡，她慢慢看出來有很多人走來走去。
(At that time) she was at home. She gradually noticed that there were many people walk around.

S.FORM: ho mohcu biavovei ho isi cu aiti ho mo toa 'ocea to iachi ua.
S.TRANSL: 然後她看到(那些人)在採自己家的茶園。
Ane then she saw that (people) were picking tea leaves of (the yard) of her (family).

S.FORM: a'ʉmtʉ na'no koicʉ ho imza o'te eʉsvʉta ho mo ho mimza iachi toa 'ocea.
S.TRANSL: 真的是罵得很凶，因為我們沒有告訴他我們自己在採茶。
(She) scolded severely, because we didn't tell him that we picked tea leaves on our own.

S.FORM: imza 'so ac'o peecveni ho eninca te X c'o ahʉyʉ mici maezo mʉpʉtʉ XeevohoX cu aupucio meuakhago ho meecunu.
S.TRANSL: 我們當初欺騙他是說不要告訴他，不然他會跟著想要做, 然後趕緊抓一個籃子跟著去。
We cheated her (on this) because we didn't want to let her know. Otherwise, (she) would follow, and hastily grabbed a basket in hand (to go picking tea leaves).

S.FORM: ho te la noe'oci.
S.TRANSL: 當要依靠XXX。
When (one) wants to rely on, ....

S.FORM: aupuciva anga humameoza.
S.TRANSL: 太早把我看得太老。
It's still too early to see me as aged women.

S.FORM: ho la toa 'ocea 'o ina 'o 'o pu 'o.
S.TRANSL: XXX 平常在採茶的時候，
When XXX is picking tea leaves,

S.FORM: cu c'o iachi to mo 'a la no ako eemo'ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 也是常常獨自一個人為了什麼事情而罵人。
(She) herself usually complains.

S.FORM: hoN ho la o'te ho lamza na o'te peela pafiho.
S.TRANSL: 當我們也是要採茶的時候，沒有辦法讓他跟我們做。
When we are going to pick tea leaves, we can't make her not to go with us.

S.FORM: eh micu 'so a'ʉmtʉ mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 真的是已經很老了。
(She)'s really old.

S.FORM: zou 'a no eni 'o la ta'to'tohʉngva.
S.TRANSL: 對，在想的在擔心的事情就是這個。
Yes, it is this that (people) worry about.

S.FORM: 'ana mo sokoeva.
S.TRANSL: 擔心(她的)安全。
worried about her.

S.FORM: zou yoyayokeo.
S.TRANSL: 身體已經不是那麼靈活了。
(Her body movement) is not so agile.

S.FORM: gen gen genki ci mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 健康的老人。
... energetic old person.

S.FORM: lea n'a asngʉcʉ mimo.
S.TRANSL: 她還在喝酒嗎？
Does she still drink?

S.FORM: lea sio.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: las la la'u aiti ho lamza uh ne'e la'u 'so la c'u.
S.TRANSL: 當我們去那邊的時候我看她...
When we went there I saw her...

S.FORM: PSW: uh manci la 'so na'no genki.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼會那麼健康？
Why is she so energetic?

S.FORM: isi honci iachi ake'a eapsezuta 'a la no iachi uh to soobai iachi.
S.TRANSL: 如果口袋裡有一點錢，她會自己去店鋪。
If (she) has some money in pocket, she goes to store on herself.

S.FORM: osi la afu'a na'no iachi maeo.
S.TRANSL: （她）自己拿(米酒)。
(She) takes (rice wine) on her own.

S.FORM: Xia la ho as 'a s'aX zou uespi na lasi ma asonʉ o'a.
S.TRANSL: 她自己喜歡的是uespi嗎？可能不是吧。
Wespi is what she (likes)? It might not be...

S.FORM: uh 'a s'a anou 'a isi.
S.TRANSL: 不止, 他全部喜歡...
Not just that, she (likes all kinds).

S.FORM: X acʉha i'o mo sumu.
S.TRANSL: (那些酒)都是烈的的。
(Those liquors) are all strong.

S.FORM: oh la omosiloi ho la uh to mise zou.
S.TRANSL: 每當她去那個店鋪的時候很好笑。
It is funny when she goes to the shop.

S.FORM: la mo la mo la mo'pou .
S.TRANSL: 她經常拿錯。
(She) often wrongly took...

S.FORM: 'o mo eon to to bauteping ci vijiu.
S.TRANSL: (她是想拿)在保特瓶的米酒。
(She wanted to take) the rice wine in plastic bottle.

S.FORM: X ci la miho buhpou ho mayo to chumu.
S.TRANSL: 她有時候看錯又拿到水。
(She) sometimes read wrongly and took water.

S.FORM: 'a la maine'e ho tasi cu.
S.TRANSL: 回家，然後就..
(She) went home and then...

S.FORM: paavi ho peotoma.
S.TRANSL: (她)打開然後喝看看。
(She) opens it and has a sip.

S.FORM: osi la na aiti to inoconi mo ausuhcu man'i 'o 'o chumu ci mo yon to cingona to hifi.
S.TRANSL: 後來嬸嬸看到屋角後面怎麼有越來越多的水。
Aunt saw there were more and more (bottled) water in the back of a corner of the house.

S.FORM: ia ia iama 'o lasi 'o lasi ucia i ima hon iama chumu lasi cu lasi cu soyafa.
S.TRANSL: 他原來喝的是水，就把他擺在外面。
Actually, what she drand is water. She just placed them outside.

S.FORM: cu XpatoyangzaX cu man'i.
S.TRANSL: 排在外面的已經很多了。
What were put outside have been so many.

S.FORM: All: Xalʉ ahʉyʉX uh ta emoo ho eunu no peothomʉ ne moso n'a maaya.
S.TRANSL: 何必講到家裡，講到試喝這個事情，過去還在日本時代，
How come refers to our home, regarding having a sip on something. In the passed Japanese period,...

S.FORM: la acni yu'eteuyunu 'e lijia ho leye lah ho la untokai.
S.TRANSL: 每次運動會, 里佳和樂野都要聚在一起。
Whenever sport competition, people from Lichia and Leye gathered together.

S.FORM: zou cu smothomʉ.
S.TRANSL: 就比賽(射擊)。
(They) competed (in shooting).

S.FORM: eahoovi 'o 'o pasu 'e yusvi ne yo'hunge.
S.TRANSL: 很愛講話的那個在yo'hunge的pasuya有拿到獎。
The talkative Pasuya in Yo'hunge got award (in that competition).

S.FORM: 'o nia amo pasu 'e yo'hunge.
S.TRANSL: 以前我在yo'hunge那邊的pasuya叔叔(叔伯輩的)。
My past uncle Pasuya in Yo'hunge.

S.FORM: ma te'o c'o ak'i.
S.TRANSL: 我要叫他阿公。
I should call him grandfather.

S.FORM: cu maine'e na eanelika.
S.TRANSL: 里佳的人回去了。
People from Lika returned.

S.FORM: cu mito 'a moso c'o 'so la XnioX toyovucu to to ea nia la kuici ceoa 'o ceonʉ ho mo'unu maita'e.
S.TRANSL: 他們走到... 因為以前那個路是從..從墳墓旁邊走下去, 就這樣走。
(They) walked till... Because in the past, (people) usually moved on the road downward by the grave area.

S.FORM: micu n'a asonʉ esmi to peohnasi.
S.TRANSL: (他們)可能已經走到那個（應該指墳墓）下面。
(They) probably had been below the grave area.

S.FORM: haah'o haah tosvo n'a.
S.TRANSL: (他喊)大家先休息一下！
(He shouted) Everybody! Stop for a while!

S.FORM: te'o n'a i'ima 'o hovi'u.
S.TRANSL: 我找一下我的獎品。
Allow me to find my award.

S.FORM: mo n'a yon to mo kahkʉmnʉ ci pania.
S.TRANSL: 在那個很粗的瓶子裡面。
(my award) is still in the thick bottle.

S.FORM: isi cu na peothoma.
S.TRANSL: 他據說就喝看看。
He as reported just had a sip.

S.FORM: ah ci ma sooyu.
S.TRANSL: 原來是醬油。
Unexpectedly, it's soy sauce.

S.FORM: WS: taunano s'a emi.
S.TRANSL: 以為是酒。
(I) thought (it was) wine.

S.FORM: WS: hacucu haah'o.
S.TRANSL: 大家，走了走了！
Getgoing! Everybody!

S.FORM: c'o eh mimo ta sooyu.
S.TRANSL: 只有喝到醬油而已。
(He) only drank soy sauce.

S.FORM: isi sia to pania ci mo na'no kahkʉmnʉ 'o sooyu.
S.TRANSL: 醬油就裝在一個很粗的瓶子裡面。
Soy sauce was filled in a very thick bottle.

S.FORM: hoN ye o'ano hoovisi ho mo smothomʉ.
S.TRANSL: 就是他射擊比賽的獎品。
That was the prize of the shooting competition for him.

S.FORM: zou c'o osnia tauna'no emi na mo yon no pania.
S.TRANSL: 馬上就以為在瓶子裡的東西是酒。
(He) thought immediately what in the bottle is wine.

S.FORM: isi tauna'no emi.
S.TRANSL: 他以為是酒。
He thought it was wine

S.FORM: 'a isi cohivi c'o sooyu.
S.TRANSL: 他不知道是醬油。
He didn't know it's just soy sauce.

S.FORM: ma sooyu.
S.TRANSL: 醬油喔。
Unexcpectedly, soy sauce.

S.FORM: 'a isi cu no isi cu no peothoma.
S.TRANSL: 他才喝看看。
He just had a sip.

S.FORM: zou asngʉcʉ aveoveoyʉ na mo maica.
S.TRANSL: （反問）他這樣還會一直感到高興嗎？
Even so, (did he) still (feel) happy?

S.FORM: PG: hoovi 'ia moso la mainenu ak'i na moso la.
S.TRANSL: 阿公那個事情以前到底是...？
What was the real situation about that story in the past?

S.FORM: na moso la mah no moso la nʉthʉ uh ne fungkiko pofʉngʉ no fʉisʉ na la angʉ yusvi.
S.TRANSL: 他們帶..一起走去奮起湖，用頭帶背著米，那時候太多話。
(They) brought... and went together to fungkiko. (The garrulous grandfather) carried rice.

S.FORM: angʉ yusvi.
S.TRANSL: （他）太嘮叨。
(He) was too garrulous.

S.FORM: nte ci nte yusvi 'o mo kaebʉ ya'ei.
S.TRANSL: 很喜歡講話。
(He) liked speaking.

S.FORM: yusvi 'o 'o nia na amo pasu 'e eayovea.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼會那麼多話 Eayovea那邊的pasuya叔叔。
Why uncle Pasuya in eayovea was so garrulous.

S.FORM: ea zou mo nana SGW: SGW: oh mo nana nʉthʉ na s seito ne kosiuso.
S.TRANSL: 聽說是和國習所的學生一起走。
It's reported that (he) went together with elementary school students.

S.FORM: mhm panto nia 'so la ceonʉ moso la mineomza.
S.TRANSL: 以前有條路就是從上面走。
There was a road. (That is, people) walked from the above path.

S.FORM: oh panto nia la kei kiukeizo.
S.TRANSL: 以前那邊有一個休息區。
There was a place for rest.

S.FORM: te n'a tosvo na'a.
S.TRANSL: 先休息一下。
Let's take a rest first.

S.FORM: na a'ʉmtʉ to'sina'o na 'o'oko ho.
S.TRANSL: 那些小孩好像真的休息很久，而且...
Those children were reported to stop for a long time, and...

S.FORM: 'a monna acʉhʉ mahafo.
S.TRANSL: 不是所有人都有帶東西啦。
Not all of them carried some stuff.

S.FORM: monna pofngu no fʉisʉ 'o nia amo pasuya.
S.TRANSL: 以前那個pasuya叔叔那時正用頭帶背米。
The past uncle Pasuya was packing rice with the head belt.

S.FORM: tosvo cu nana.
S.TRANSL: 然後聽說就...
Then, it was reported...

S.FORM: mainca na 'o'oko te c'u e'ohʉ.
S.TRANSL: 小孩子就講說, 我們走了。
The children said, let's move on.

S.FORM: cu acʉhʉ mo cu aʉlʉ mo na'o.
S.TRANSL: 全部真的已經走了一段時間。
(They all) really had walked for a while.

S.FORM: nna mainca 'o nia amo pasuya.
S.TRANSL: 聽說那個amo pasuya講說...
It's reported that Uncle Pasuya said...

S.FORM: 'ia ma s'a i'o os'o hafa.
S.TRANSL: 啊，我帶的東西呢。
Ah, I didn't bring something with me.

S.FORM: SWP: Xta'payo'aX na cono sava.
S.TRANSL: 忘記拿一麻袋的米。
(I) forgot one sack (of rice).

S.FORM: all: mo n'a aʉmtʉ ake'i.
S.TRANSL: 還真的有一點...
Indeed, somewhat....

S.FORM: mo n'a toevovei ho i'ima.
S.TRANSL: 還跑回去找(那袋米)。
He even run back and looked for (the sack of rice).

S.FORM: 'a no fʉisʉ to seito.
S.TRANSL: 那個講習所學生的米。
The rice was for elementary school students.

S.FORM: zou to moso la tmopsʉ ne.
S.TRANSL: 那些以前在（講習所）讀書的。那時...
(Those who) was studing in Kosiuso. At the time....

S.FORM: ma moso n'a maaya oh oh.
S.TRANSL: 那時候還是日本時代。
It was still Janpanese colonial period.

S.FORM: moso n'a maaya moso 'so la yon ne.
S.TRANSL: 那時候還是日本時代。因為那時候在...
It was still Japanese colonial period. Because it was at...

S.FORM: koshiuso.
S.TRANSL: 講習所。
Koshiuso.

S.FORM: nia la nongyo moso yon ne'e 'o.
S.TRANSL: 那時...在那邊。
(Someone) was there.

S.FORM: i'mi no hiasi yusvi 'o at'ingi ako smoeciciei ho ta'payo'a na.
S.TRANSL: 他那個愛講話的程度，只顧著一直講話然後把..忘記了。
How garruolous he was. He solely talked continuously, and forgot....

S.FORM: ci 'a mo nana no asngʉcʉ ea'ei ho ha'o to ha'o to'ofeihni ako eunu maita'e micu nʉthʉ na 'o'oko.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣跟小孩子一起走。
Then walked together with children.

S.FORM: ha'o to'ofeihni no 'o'oko.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣跟著走。
He just walked along with (the children).

S.FORM: yangaevi ho nʉthʉ na 'o'oko.
S.TRANSL: 一邊講話一邊跟小孩子一起走。
He talked and walked together with children.

S.FORM: ak peiskameosʉ.
S.TRANSL: （他）突然做...
(He) suddenly did...

S.FORM: 'ia mas'a i'o hiafa'u hiafa'u ta'payo'a na cono sava.
S.TRANSL: 我突然想到, 啊，對啦，我帶的東西。(我)忘了我那袋（米）。
(I) suddenly thought of what I brought with. (I) forgot one sack (of rice).

S.FORM: GP: is 'a ucia cocvi ci maica ci mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 像這樣老人家....很令人想笑。
The senior like this.. really hilarious.

S.FORM: mo mo moso la aʉmtʉ maica 'o o'u la talʉi ak'i.
S.TRANSL: 以前我所聽到的事情真的是這樣嗎？ 阿公？
Is what I heard before true? Grandfather?

S.FORM: oa na moso la supihi ne c'oeha ho.
S.TRANSL: 聽說那人曾經涉水而過曾文溪。
It's reported that the guy once waded across Cenwun creek.

S.FORM: pofngu no fʉisʉ ho mʉpʉtʉ no emi.
S.TRANSL: 頭背著米手拿著酒。
(He) carried rice on (his) head, and held wine (in hand).

S.FORM: mon na supihi mon na smopcuku.
S.TRANSL: 他涉水而過跌倒。
(He) waded across the creek, and fell.

S.FORM: moso la aʉmtʉ maica.
S.TRANSL: 真的是這樣？
Was it really like this?

S.FORM: 'a os'o s'a cohivi.
S.TRANSL: 我不知道。
I don't know.

S.FORM: emoemova eni.
S.TRANSL: 這個講得太誇張了。
This has been exaggerately talked.

S.FORM: ma tac'u.
S.TRANSL: 差不多了。
OK. We should stop here.
